articles.title: Gov. Healey backs bill to keep Mass. bars open until 3 a.m. this summer
articles.description:
The legislation would allow licensed establishments to sell alcohol one hour later than their normal closing time, up to 3 a.m., between June 1 and Aug. 31, 2026.

The post Gov. Healey backs bill to keep Mass. bars open until 3 a.m. this summer appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Gov. Healey supports extending bar hours to 3 a.m. from June 1 to August 31, 2026.
The bill allows alcohol sales one hour later during summer months for licensed establishments.
Boston nightlife may see increased revenue and tourism with extended hours for bars.

articles.title: The show will go on: White House correspondents’ dinner rescheduled for July, with Trump attending
articles.description:
The dinner, cut short in April by a gunman who prosecutors say was trying to assassinate Trump, will now take place on July 24.

The post The show will go on: White House correspondents’ dinner rescheduled for July, with Trump attending appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: White House correspondents' dinner rescheduled for July 24 after April incident.
Trump will attend the event, highlighting ongoing political tensions.
Boston residents may see increased security and media presence during the event.

articles.title: A.J. Brown makes his Patriots practice debut and other takeaways from Day 2 of OTAs
articles.description:
"Tying to adjust, walking up the hill with my uniform on, I was like 'man, this is real," Brown said of his practice debut.

The post A.J. Brown makes his Patriots practice debut and other takeaways from Day 2 of OTAs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A.J. Brown debuted at Patriots OTAs on June 2, showcasing his skills to fans.
Brown expressed excitement about joining the team, emphasizing the significance of his practice debut.
His performance may enhance the Patriots' offensive strategy for the upcoming season.

articles.title: Market Basket’s ex-CEO, Arthur T. Demoulas, says he won’t fight court ruling upholding his firing
articles.description:
“Mr. Demoulas will continue in his efforts to set right what has gone so wrong here,” a spokesperson for the ousted Market Basket leader said.

The post Market Basket’s ex-CEO, Arthur T. Demoulas, says he won’t fight court ruling upholding his firing appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Arthur T. Demoulas will not appeal his court-ordered firing from Market Basket.
Demoulas aims to address issues stemming from his ousting, affecting Market Basket's operations.
Local employees and customers may experience changes in leadership and store management.

articles.title: MBTA and Boston announce plans for Summer Street closures during World Cup
articles.description:
After some back-and-forth between the state and city entities, the two have come up with a plan.

The post MBTA and Boston announce plans for Summer Street closures during World Cup appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MBTA and Boston will close Summer Street for World Cup events in 2026.
Closures aim to enhance safety and accessibility for thousands of expected attendees.
Residents should prepare for traffic disruptions and plan alternate routes during events.

articles.title: Man allegedly involved in wrong-way Route 1 crash detained by ICE before arraignment
articles.description:
A Newburyport man was released on bail after the crash on Sunday, which injured a State Police trooper. ICE agents detained him outside a courthouse Tuesday.

The post Man allegedly involved in wrong-way Route 1 crash detained by ICE before arraignment appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A Newburyport man was involved in a wrong-way crash on Route 1, injuring a State Police trooper.
The man was released on bail after the crash on Sunday, June 2.
ICE detained him outside the courthouse before his arraignment, raising concerns among local residents.

articles.title: UConn-Michigan: Hall of Fame Series is returning to Boston’s TD Garden
articles.description:
The Hall of Fame Series at Boston's TD Garden will feature UConn for the third straight year, as well as national champs Michigan.

The post UConn-Michigan: Hall of Fame Series is returning to Boston’s TD Garden appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: UConn and Michigan will compete in the Hall of Fame Series at TD Garden.
This marks the third consecutive year UConn participates in this event.
Local fans can expect increased attendance and excitement at Boston's TD Garden.

articles.title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in June
articles.description:
A full list of what to watch streaming on Netflix, Hulu, Prime Video, HBO Max, Disney+, Paramount+, Apple TV, and Peacock in June 2026.

The post All the new movies and TV shows streaming in June appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Discover new streaming options available in June 2026 for Boston residents.
Major platforms include Netflix, Hulu, and Disney+ with diverse content.
Local viewers can enjoy fresh movies and shows enhancing summer entertainment.

articles.title: Police looking to ID man after stabbing outside South Boston dive bar
articles.description:
The stabbing occurred early Sunday morning.

The post Police looking to ID man after stabbing outside South Boston dive bar appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police investigate a stabbing incident outside a South Boston dive bar early Sunday morning.
Victim's identity remains undisclosed; police seek public assistance in identifying the suspect.
Residents urged to stay vigilant as police enhance patrols in the area following the incident.

articles.title: Yankee Candle founder’s estate sells for far below asking price, will become ‘luxury getaway’
articles.description:
A PGA golf pro and his wife bought the Western Massachusetts compound for $4.55 million after it spent years on the market with a $23 million asking price.

The post Yankee Candle founder’s estate sells for far below asking price, will become ‘luxury getaway’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Yankee Candle founder's estate sold for $4.55 million after years on the market.
The property was initially listed for $23 million, indicating a significant price drop.
The new owners plan to convert the estate into a luxury getaway, impacting local tourism.
